installations.name: NHARNG State Military Reservation (HIMARS HQ)
installations.blurb: Battalion headquarters of the 3rd Battalion, 197th Field Artillery (M142 HIMARS) plus Joint Force HQ-NH and the 12th Civil Support Team — the command brain of the East. The launchers home-station out of town, but the order to fire originates here, giving the East the longest reach on the map.

installations.name: Army Aviation Support Facility (Black Hawks)
installations.blurb: The AASF at Concord Municipal Airport bases both the 1-169th Aviation lift Black Hawks (UH-60L) and the C/3-238th MEDEVAC fleet (HH-60M). This rotary-wing lift is the East's single best-supported advantage — it lets the East bypass every contested river bridge entirely.

installations.name: Concord Municipal Airport
installations.blurb: The only hard-surface runway in the city and the physical home of the East's air mobility. Fuel, hangars, and a 6,000-ft runway make it the staging point for both Guard aviation and any resupply that arrives by air rather than over the bridges.

installations.name: NH Supreme Court / State Office Park East
installations.blurb: The judicial and executive nerve center sitting on the East side of the line: the NH Supreme Court, the Department of Safety HQ (parent of State Police), and the high ground of State Office Park East. Holding it gives the East legal legitimacy plus a fortified administrative complex.

stats.label: Tenants
stats.value: Supreme Court, DOS HQ, state offices

installations.name: NH State Data Center / Dept. of Safety HQ (33 Hazen Dr)
installations.blurb: The state's primary data center (Dept. of Information Technology) and State Police HQ dispatch sit on the I-93/State Office Park seam — physically straddling the front line. Whoever holds it controls statewide communications, dispatch, and the E911 backbone.

installations.name: Steeplegate Mall (staging fortress)
installations.blurb: A dead megastructure on the Heights with acres of roofed floor and parking. In the war it converts into the East's barracks, motor pool, and forward staging area — a ready-made fortified node behind the I-93 line.

installations.name: Loudon Road logistics belt
installations.blurb: The Loudon Rd big-box corridor — Walmart, Sam's Club, Target, Market Basket — is the East's quartermaster. Warehouse-scale food, fuel, and dry goods sit pre-stocked east of the river, feeding a siege from the inside.

installations.name: Rymes Propane & Oil bulk plant
installations.blurb: A regional propane and heating-oil bulk depot at 257 Sheep Davis Rd, well east of I-93. It is the East's on-side fuel reserve — bulk heating and vehicle fuel that the West cannot reach without crossing the line.

installations.name: Old Turnpike Rd landfill solar (6.7 MW)
installations.blurb: A 6.7 MW DC array of ~11,000 panels atop the capped city landfill — local generation independent of the grid. The site geocodes near the I-93 seam, so it is flagged contested rather than safely East; verify the array footprint before claiming it.

installations.name: Oak Hill (eastern high ground)
installations.blurb: The dominant terrain northeast of the city. Whoever holds Oak Hill owns the viewshed: its line of sight reaches deep across the west bank, turning East observation into East fire on roughly a third of West Concord's ground.

installations.name: NH State House
installations.blurb: The oldest state capitol in the U.S. still seating its legislature in its original chambers — the symbolic seat of New Hampshire. The West holds the legitimacy of government even as the East holds the guns.

installations.name: Concord Hospital
installations.blurb: The region's only Level II trauma center, with a rooftop helipad and ~295 beds. In a siege the West controls casualty care for both banks — the body that can heal itself while the East must fly its wounded out.

installations.name: NH Hospital (state psychiatric)
installations.blurb: The state psychiatric hospital, ~168 locked beds, on the West bank. A secondary medical and secure-facility asset that adds to the West's care capacity and population responsibilities under siege.

stats.label: Operator
stats.value: NH Dept. of Health & Human Services

installations.name: NH State Prison for Men
installations.blurb: The West's 'dungeon' — a max/medium/minimum prison with operational capacity near 1,408. It is a huge confined population guarded by a chronically understaffed force (roughly half of corrections posts vacant), making it the war's best siege plot hook.

installations.name: NH Correctional Facility for Women
installations.blurb: The state women's prison, part of the West-bank DOC complex. Like the men's facility it concentrates a large confined population behind thin guard numbers — manpower the West must feed and contain throughout any siege.

installations.name: Concord Police HQ + Lenco BearCat
installations.blurb: City PD downtown, home to the Central NH Special Operations Unit's Lenco BearCat armored vehicle — the only armored asset in Concord and it is West. It is the West's single counter to an armored blitz across the bridges.

installations.name: Penacook Lake + Hutchins WTP (drinking water)
installations.blurb: The city's drinking-water source (Penacook Lake, ~1.5 billion gallons) and the Hutchins St treatment plant (10 MGD design) — the 'water in' for the whole city, and both sit West. The West controls who drinks.

installations.name: Hall Street Wastewater Treatment Plant
installations.blurb: The main wastewater plant (125 Hall St, ~10.1 MGD design) — the city's 'water out,' discharging to the Merrimack. With both the source and the sewer on the West bank, the East depends on West-controlled water infrastructure.

installations.name: Garvins Falls Hydroelectric Station
installations.blurb: A 12.4 MW four-unit hydro dam sitting mid-river on the Bow/Concord line — the contested generator that neither bank can fully claim because it is the river itself. The southern river gate's power prize.

installations.name: Unitil substations (Bridge St + McGuire St)
installations.blurb: Power distribution control sits West: the 60-year-old Bridge St downtown switching hub plus the McGuire St substation (being rebuilt to absorb its load) and Unitil's Capital Region operations HQ. The East has generation; the West has the grid's control room.

installations.name: Liberty Utilities / EnergyNorth gas HQ
installations.blurb: The Concord gas-distribution office (116 N Main St) for Liberty/EnergyNorth, serving ~98,000 customers up the I-93 corridor. Note: gas is a chokepoint, not a local resource — Concord is fed entirely by the Concord Lateral pipeline from Dracut MA.

installations.name: NHTI + McAuliffe-Shepard Discovery Center
installations.blurb: The community college campus and planetarium on Institute Dr — an institutional strongpoint in the contested strip between the river and I-93. Open ground, dormitories, and buildings make it a natural foothold for either bank.

installations.name: Everett Arena / Fort Eddy Rd plaza
installations.blurb: The Everett Arena and Fort Eddy Rd commercial seam sit on the east-bank edge of the contested zone, opposite downtown. It is a river-assault crossing point and a strongpoint commanding the approach off Main Street.

installations.name: The Six Merrimack River Crossings
installations.blurb: Six spans tie the two banks together — and every one is a contested choke point. The 1915 Sewalls Falls Bridge is structurally deficient and weight-limited, the fragile northern flank; the four central spans (twin I-93, I-393/US-4, Loudon Rd, Manchester St) are the high-traffic gates the whole war is fought over.

stats.label: Crossings
stats.value: 6 (Sewalls Falls, 2x I-93, I-393, Loudon Rd, Manchester St)

installations.name: The I-93 Interchanges (the front line)
installations.blurb: Interstate 93 IS the front line, and its interchanges are the gateways through it. Exit 17 (Penacook, the northern supply prize), Exit 16 (the northern breakthrough point), and the Bow Junction I-93/I-89 hinge to the south are the contested doors in the wall.

installations.name: Merrimack County Superior Court / Sheriff
installations.blurb: The county court and the Merrimack County Sheriff's Office downtown (163 N Main St) — civil process, transports, warrants, and court security on the West bank. A secondary West-side law-and-order node alongside the State House.

installations.name: Tanks / tracked armor
installations.blurb: Tracked armor at the NH National Guard State Military Reservation (reported on-site) - the heaviest direct-fire ground assets in the city, and East's armored fist.
installations.source_url: NH National Guard State Military Reservation, 1 Minuteman Way, Concord
